value: AIM UI is now a fully static site (v0.2.0). No Express server. All crypto happens in-browser using @qntm/client directly. Keys stored in localStorage. Buffer polyfill in main.tsx for cbor-x. Backup/restore via Settings page. Recipes bundled as static JSON import from gate/recipes/starter.json.
Default dropbox is ALWAYS https://inbox.qntm.corpo.llc - never local filesystem. Both CLI and aim-ui should use this by default.

Gateway vault design insights from OpenClaw/HashiCorp Vault article: (1) Secrets should have TTLs (15min/60min/4hr) based on data classification - reduces prompt injection attack surface from 'always' to limited window. (2) Gateway should NOT be able to refresh its own vault tokens - human controls token refresh. (3) Never store secrets in plaintext in config, chat history, or memory. (4) Per-secret revocation capability. (5) Separate root/bot vault users - root stores secrets, bot only reads with time-limited tokens. (6) Human-in-the-loop for token refresh is intentionally cumbersome but secure.

Go CLI is deprecated. TypeScript and Python clients are the priority implementations. Test-first approach: write tests before code. Keep TS client (client/) and Python client (python-dist/) in sync with gate changes.
